immediate: Unplug non-essential electronics and avoid using wired phones.
Stay indoors and avoid contact with metal fixtures and water.
Do not touch electrical panels or exposed wiring during the storm.
preventive: Install lightning arrestors and surge protectors on main distribution panels.
Ensure proper earthing/grounding of all major electrical installations.
Avoid tall isolated structures near antennae or external wiring.
emergency: If a strike causes sparks or fire, isolate the supply if safe and call emergency services.
Keep distance from downed power lines and report to the Ceylon Electricity Board immediately.

immediate: Avoid operating electrical devices with wet hands or near water ingress points.
Cover external outlets and elevate sensitive electronics if water is rising.
preventive: Use waterproof covers for outdoor sockets and junction boxes.
Inspect roof and drainage before monsoon season to reduce infiltration near electrics.
emergency: If water enters distribution boards, do not touch; shut off supply at main breaker if safe and call a professional.
Avoid turning power back on until a qualified electrician verifies safety.

immediate: Turn off mains power and disconnect appliances if safe to do so before water reaches electrical points.
Move portable electronics and essential appliances to higher ground.
preventive: Elevate critical equipment and sockets above expected flood levels.
Install ELCBs/RCDs to reduce electrocution risk when water ingress occurs.
emergency: Do not attempt to restore power to submerged equipment — a full inspection is required.
Contact licensed electricians and your electricity provider for post-flood checks.

immediate: Secure or bring inside loose electrical appliances and outdoor cables.
Keep away from fallen lines and report faults to relevant authorities.
preventive: Trim trees near overhead lines and secure rooftop installations.
Ensure antennas and satellite dishes are properly mounted.
emergency: If power poles or conductors fall, do not approach and keep a safe perimeter until professionals arrive.
Switch off mains if there is visible damage to home wiring and call a licensed electrician.

before: Prepare an evacuation plan and move valuables and electronics to higher ground.
Install ELCBs and ensure main breaker access is clear.
during: Never touch electrical equipment while standing in water.
Turn off mains supply if it can be done safely and avoid using electrical appliances.
after: Do not switch on appliances that have been submerged until professionally checked.
Have distribution boards and wiring inspected by a licensed electrician before restoring power.

before: Unplug non-essential electronics and secure outdoor items that may attract lightning.
Ensure surge protection and earthing are in place for important systems.
during: Stay indoors, avoid baths or showers, and do not use wired phones.
Keep away from windows and external metal fixtures.
after: Wait 30 minutes after the last thunder before using sensitive equipment.
Inspect antennas and external wiring for physical damage.

before: Clear gutters and drains to reduce water pooling near electrical outlets.
Protect outdoor distribution boards with waterproof enclosures.
during: Avoid using electrical appliances near water and monitor for leaks near switches and sockets.
Power down non-essential circuits if water ingress is suspected.
after: Dry and inspect affected areas before reconnecting power.
Engage a professional to test residual current devices and grounding.

before: Secure outdoor equipment and disconnect non-essential electrical supplies.
Backup critical electronics with UPS and ensure emergency lighting is available.
during: Stay away from windows and external electrical fittings; keep flashlight and battery packs ready.
Avoid contact with any exposed wiring or fallen cables.
after: Wait for authorities to declare it safe before restoring major power loads.
Have a full inspection of wiring, masts and connections before re-energizing.

general: Keep fridge elevated on stable stands where minor flooding is possible.
Use surge protectors for sensitive compressors and electronic controls.
duringThunderstorm: Unplug refrigerator if lightning risk is severe and you expect long outages.
Avoid plugging in until mains are stable after the storm.
duringFlood: Do not attempt to power on a refrigerator that has been submerged; have it inspected.
Discard food if the appliance lost power for long periods unless temperature was maintained.

general: Ensure outdoor units are secured and electrical isolators are elevated.
Use licensed technicians for installation and earthing checks.
duringThunderstorm: Switch off and unplug if lightning activity is high and storms are nearby.
duringFlood: Do not operate split-system outdoor units if water levels are high; isolate power and consult a technician.

general: Install ELCB/RCD protection for water heater circuits.
Avoid using if there are signs of moisture ingress or after storms until inspected.
duringThunderstorm: Do not use electric showers or water heaters during a thunderstorm if lightning risk is present.
duringFlood: Turn off and isolate water heaters if water levels reach electrical fittings; do not touch when wet.

general: Keep washing machines elevated where possible and ensure supply cords are dry before use.
Use ELCBs on circuits that feed wet areas.
duringThunderstorm: Avoid running washing machines during heavy lightning activity to reduce risk from surges.
duringFlood: Do not operate if the socket or machine base is wet; have unit serviced before reuse.

general: Use surge protection and unplug antennas during severe storms.
Avoid connecting via long exposed antenna cables; use surge arrestors where needed.
duringThunderstorm: Unplug TV and external antenna during storms to avoid damage from lightning strikes.
duringFlood: Do not power on if TV or stand has been in contact with water until professionally checked.

general: Use UPS for graceful shutdowns and surge protection for sensitive equipment.
Disconnect ethernet and external antenna lines during storms to reduce surge paths.
duringThunderstorm: Shut down and unplug desktop computers; keep laptops on battery if needed and unplug chargers.
duringFlood: Do not attempt to dry and power on computers that were submerged; seek professional data recovery if needed.

electricalEmergency: If you encounter exposed live conductors or sparking equipment, keep clear and call CEB faults immediately.
Do not attempt repairs on distribution equipment — wait for licensed crews.
